pi_items.source: export_result.sh line 8: OUTPUT_CSV="/home/ga/reports/catchment_analysis.csv"; setup_task.sh creates /home/ga/reports directory at line 17
export_result.sh lines 16-29: SQL SELECT statement lists FchPat_CP as postal_code, age_bracket (from CASE), FchPat_Sexe as sex, COUNT(*) as patient_count

export_result.sh lines 18-24: CASE statement defines brackets as: WHEN TIMESTAMPDIFF(YEAR, FchPat_Nee, CURDATE()) <= 17 THEN '0-17', BETWEEN 18 AND 39 THEN '18-39', BETWEEN 40 AND 59 THEN '40-59', BETWEEN 60 AND 79 THEN '60-79', ELSE '80+'
setup_task.sh line 34: sexes = ['F', 'H']; used in patient data generation; confirmed in task_utils.sh line 205 comment

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified. The task generates synthetic patient data: 120 patients are created in the DrTuxTest MySQL database with random distribution across 8 French postal codes (75001, 75011, 75015, 75020, 92100, 93100, 94200, 78000), 5 age brackets (0-17, 18-39, 40-59, 60-79, 80+) with weights 15%, 30%, 30%, 20%, 5% respectively, and 2 sexes (F, H). The expected CSV output at /home/ga/reports/catchment_analysis.csv must have headers [postal_code, age_bracket, sex, patient_count] and contain cross-tabulated patient counts by postal code, age bracket, and sex.
